items.title: Queensland government pledges to restore dams in budget

children.content: The treasurer has also promised no cuts to public service, but won't commit to returning to surplus within the next four years.

gallery.altText: From above a deep lake glimmers on an overcast day, a dam wall can be seen off to the side

thumbnail.altText: From above a deep lake glimmers on an overcast day, a dam wall can be seen off to the side

items.title: Operator of troubled Callide power station sued for more than $1b

image.altText: A large hole blown in the side of an industrial building.

children.content: Foreign investment group Sev.en said a series of catastrophic failures at the Callide power station had led to 1,700 days of lost generation capacity.

items.title: Runaway Tasmanian devil Mary discharged from hospital

children.content: The marsupial sparked a desperate two-week search after escaping from a Gold Coast wildlife park.

image.altText: A wildlife park staff member holds a Tasmanian devil in her arms.

items.title: Developer allegedly squandered $10m earmarked for disability housing

children.content: Gold Coast businessman David McWilliams allegedly spent the investor funds, raised specifically for six Specialist Disability Accommodation projects, on a luxury car, high-rise, pub and crypto.

image.altText: A man wearing a red polo shirt sits at a desk holding paperwork next to a person wearing a blue checkered shirt.

items.title: Backlash as council compares rate rise to bread and cheese

children.content: Residents are frustrated as councils pass increasing costs to ratepayers already struggling with the cost-of-living crisis.

gallery.altText: A woman holding a dog, standing in front of a garden at the front of her home.

thumbnail.altText: A woman holding a dog, standing in front of a garden at the front of her home.

items.title: Man found guilty over fatal punch attack near Maryborough pub

children.content: A jury has found Declan Jamie Thomas Burton, 23, guilty of a deadly punch attack outside a tavern in Maryborough on November 2023.

image.altText: Medium-sized brick building with sign reading 'Lamington Hotel Motel'.

items.title: Bus fleet set to connect southern Gold Coast to existing light rail

children.content: The Queensland government has released the plan nine months after controversially scrapping stage 4 light rail amid community pushback.

image.altText: Artist graphic of metro style bus on a bridge over water

items.title: 2.7 tonnes of cocaine found in elaborate bunker system at Sydney property

children.content: A record 2.7 tonnes of cocaine worth an estimated street value of $816 million has been seized at a semi-rural property in Greater Western Sydney.

gallery.altText: A series of plastic tubs filled with packaged drugs sitting inside a hole within a shipping container floor.

thumbnail.altText: A series of plastic tubs filled with packaged drugs sitting inside a hole within a shipping container floor.

items.title: Kennedy looks for sprint double as Comm Games athletics team revealed

children.content: Australia's newly flush sprinting stocks squeeze Josh Azzopardi out of the solo events despite finishing second in the 100m at the national championships in April.

image.altText: Sprinter Lachlan Kennedy smiles and points at the camera with two outstretched hands

items.title: Queensland watch houses overcrowded nearly a year after internal review

children.content: The Queensland Police Union says chronic overcrowding is still putting officers and prisoners at risk, despite government pledges to overhaul the system.

image.altText: Sign outside Brisbane watch house sign warning "no entry"

items.title: Young people with disability 'devastated' as charity closes employment program

children.content: Wesley Mission Queensland will close its employment service for young people with disability due to the "increasingly complex" NDIS environment.

gallery.altText: A young man with glasses in a bright green shirt next to another man in a blue shirt.

thumbnail.altText: A young man with glasses in a bright green shirt next to another man in a blue shirt.

avatar.alt: A woman wearing a jacket sits in a park.

items.title: Macadamia orchard area expands up east coast, doubling in size

children.content: The macadamia production area in Australia has more than doubled in the past decade, but the price is expected to hold strong for consumers.

image.altText: Workers hand-sorting macadamias on a conveyer belt.

items.title: Drones and AI combine to help bring birds back from the brink

children.content: Birds are going extinct at an alarming rate across the globe, but Australian researchers say technology could help turn the tide.

items.title: Dry winter conditions forecast for Queensland before rain returns

children.content: After a week of unseasonal rainfall, much of the state will return to typical winter conditions from Tuesday, with drier, cooler mornings and sunny afternoons.

image.altText: couple on ebikes looking towards ocean horizon

items.title: Boy, 12, dies when allegedly stolen car flips in Cairns

children.content: Police say the car was reported stolen an hour before the accident at around 4am.

image.altText: A car on its roof beside a road in Cairns.

items.title: When US troops arrived in Brisbane during World War II, Aussies were soon 'kicking the hell out of them'

children.content: The American war effort made Brisbane its stronghold in 1942, and thousands of attractive American soldiers were situated in Brisbane. That didn't sit so well with the Australians.

gallery.altText: Black and white photo of two men in military uniform standing outside a building marked "Central Hotel".
Black and white photo of US navy sailors dressed in white in 1942.
Old colour photograph of American GIs in Brisbane city in 1942.

thumbnail.altText: Black and white photo of two men in military uniform standing outside a building marked "Central Hotel".
Black and white photo of US navy sailors dressed in white in 1942.
Old colour photograph of American GIs in Brisbane city in 1942.

items.title: Changes to support regional Queensland patients a 'drop in the ocean'

children.content: The Queensland government is spruiking its cost-of-living measures in its upcoming state budget, but advocates say changes to support regional patients are disappointing.

image.altText: A woman in a green jumper leans on a fence post outside for a portrait.

items.title: Speedway plans spark protest

children.content: Residents of Beachmere in Moreton Bay say a proposed speedway track will “fundamentally change the character" of the small coastal town.

image.altText: Protesters stand behind a banner at Beachmere.

items.title: Inside the major clean-up of a river city struck by flood

children.content: Forty flood-ravaged boats and 26 missing pontoons wash up in remote places following a major flood through Bundaberg, some travelling more than 100 kilometres to the outer reef islands.

image.altText: A blond-haired woman with her arms crossed and standing in front of a red coloured yacht

items.title: The people bringing life back to Maryborough's heritage buildings

children.content: Some Maryborough residents are secretly restoring several heritage-listed buildings to their original state, transforming them in a bid to create an entertainment and dining precinct in  the regional Queensland city.

image.altText: A man with dark framed glasses smiles while leaning against a brick alcove.

items.title: Women remaining in abusive relationships over concerns for pets' safety

children.content: One woman has told the ABC she may have never left her abusive former partner if she had not been able to secure a safe home for her dog.

gallery.altText: Photos of a poodle cross dog.

thumbnail.altText: Photos of a poodle cross dog.

items.title: Fears policy change exposes farmers using own gear to fight fires

children.content: More than half of Queensland's volunteer rural fire brigades use privately owned vehicles to fight bushfires, with insurance changes leaving them to question their coverage.

image.altText: A buggy with two firefighters navigate a dry grass paddock amid a hazard burn.

items.title: Beetlejuice the Musical's Australian run ends early

children.content: Beetlejuice the Musical is calling curtains early on its Australian run, citing high operational costs.

image.altText: On stage, Eddie Perfect, in a prison-striped suit, poses, grimacing, an ensemble of similarly dressed actors around him.
